truthAudit.Summary: Run 2 identified 7 vulnerabilities. Against the 20 true vulnerabilities, 6 were found (1 fully, 5 partially with severity/CWE misassessments) and 14 were missed entirely. No findings were false positives, but several severity ratings were incorrect.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Format String Vulnerability in Debug Logger ... severity: High
Items.Notes: Run 2 found the same printf(active_format.c_str(), user_input) issue in log_debug_message but rated it High instead of Critical. The vulnerability is the same; only the severity assessment was wrong.
The factorial function (lines 312-320) overflows long long for inputs above 20, but Run 2 never mentions fact or integer overflow in arithmetic functions.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Use-After-Free in Asynchronous History Flush ... severity: High
Items.Notes: Run 2 correctly identified stage_deferred_history_flush queuing lambdas holding raw pool pointers then cleanup() freeing the pool. However it rated this High while the true severity is Critical.
Run 2 only found command injection in admin exec (execute_admin_command). It never mentions validate_config, which also builds a system() call from config-controlled strings (lines 563-568).

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Stack Buffer Overflow in evaluate_expression ... severity: Critical
Items.Notes: Run 2 found the inverted bounds check in evaluate_expression causing unbounded strcpy into the 1024-byte stack buffer. However it over-assessed severity as Critical while the true severity is High.
ThreadSafeCounter reads value_ before acquiring the lock (line 483), a classic race condition. Run 2 never discusses the threading module's counter.

The EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE backdoor at lines 592-598 silently grants admin access after 5 failed attempts. Run 2 discusses authentication only in terms of the hardcoded secret, never mentioning the override logic.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Path Traversal in Configuration Loader ... CWE-22 ... severity: Medium
Items.Notes: Run 2 found the exact same path traversal in load_config (config_dir_ + filename with no sanitization), with matching CWE and severity.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: Heap Buffer Overflow in Expression Parser via strcpy ... CWE-120
Items.Notes: Run 2 found the same strcpy into the 256-byte expression_buffer_ but classified it CWE-120 (stack/linear buffer overflow) instead of CWE-122 (heap-based buffer overflow), which is the correct classification for a heap-allocated buffer.
Run 2 found the exact same ADMIN_SECRET compile-time constant but under-assessed severity as Medium while the true severity is High.
Session tokens are 4-digit codes generated by mt19937 (lines 635-639), trivially guessable. Run 2 never discusses session token generation.
The static cached_time in log_debug_message is updated via localtime without synchronization, a data race in multithreaded use. Run 2 never discusses the logging timestamp code.
append_override_audit_event writes to a predictable temp path via system() (lines 622-632). Run 2 never discusses this audit logging code path.
authenticated_, login_attempts_, and session_token_ persist across operations without session expiry. Run 2 never discusses session state persistence.
handle_variable_command calls std::stod(tokens[3]) at line 729 with no try/catch, allowing unhandled exceptions. Run 2 never discusses variable parsing error handling.
The block split at lines 132-133 computes previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES which can underflow for small blocks. Run 2 never discusses memory pool block splitting.
parse_expression/parse_term/parse_factor recurse with no depth limit, allowing stack exhaustion via nested parentheses. Run 2 never discusses parser recursion limits.
evaluate_cached accesses result_cache_ from thread pool tasks while the main thread may also call it, with no mutex protection on the cache map. Run 2 never discusses the result cache.
load_config checks filesystem::exists then sleeps 25ms then opens the file (lines 544-546), a textbook TOCTOU. Run 2 never discusses this timing gap.
calculate_route_score divides 100 by token_length which is 0 for empty input (line 752). Run 2 never discusses command routing.
